data.title: Stock Market News Today – SPY and QQQ Stock Inch Lower Ahead of Key U.S.-China Meeting
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and Nasdaq 100 (NDX) failed to close higher for a third consecutive day, ending the Friday session with small losses of 0.07% and 0.01%, respectively.

This morning, President Trump posted on Truth Social that an “80% Tariff on China seems right! Up to Scott B." Over the weekend, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet with top trade representatives from China in a high-stakes trade talk. Trump also added that many trade deals are in the pipeline.

China provided its own remarks ahead of the meeting, with Commerce Minister Wang Wentao saying that China opposes the U.S. abusing the use of reciprocal tariffs.

Meanwhile, the Federal Bank Reserve of New York released its latest Nowcast GDP update, forecasting second quarter growth of 2.42%, up from 2.34% a week ago.

In other news, the U.S. is considering entering into a Compact of Free Association (COFA) agreement with Greenland, according to Reuters. While details are still developing, the agreement typically includes benefits like mail services, military protection, and duty-free trade on a varying scale. It also provides the U.S. with the power to set up its military on the COFA partner’s land. The Trump administration is still weighing its options with no decision set in stone yet.

The S&P 500 finished the trading week with a 0.12% loss while the Nasdaq 100 moved higher by 0.31%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Administration Mulling Nuclear Energy Executive Orders
data.description:
The Trump Administration is considering issuing executive orders in order to advance the construction of nuclear energy power plants, according to a report from The New York Times. The publication, which reviewed four drafts of the orders, notes that the administration believes that the U.S. is trailing China in terms of nuclear power progress and would like to revise federal safety regulations in order to speed up power plant construction.

Trump Administration Seeks to Boost Nuclear Power Plant Construction

One of the drafts states that 87% of nuclear reactors installed since 2017 are based on Russian and Chinese designs. “These trends cannot continue. Swift and decisive action is required to jump-start America's nuclear renaissance,” reads the draft.

The VanEck Uranium and Nuclear Energy ETF (NLR) is trading higher on the news.

Disclaimer & DisclosureReport an Issue
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Considers Special Trade Status for Greenland
data.description:
The U.S. is considering entering into a Compact of Free Association (COFA) agreement with Greenland, according to a new report from Reuters. At the moment, only Micronesia, the Marshall Islands and Palau have COFA agreements with the U.S.

What is a Compact of Free Association Agreement?

The exact benefits of being a COFA partner varies, but typically includes things like mail services, military protection, and “largely duty-free” trade. The U.S. military is also allowed to be present in COFA countries.

Sources close to the matter have stated that the U.S. is also considering alternative plans for Greenland and that a COFA agreement would be subject to political challenges. The COFA “could be an elegant way to address some of the concerns that we have when it comes to Greenland security,” an administration official told Reuters.

The S&P 500 has not shown a significant reaction to the news and is nearly unchanged on the day.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Won't Unilaterally Lower China Tariffs, Says  Press Sec. Leavitt
data.description:
Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt has emphasized that President Trump will not unilaterally lower the 145% tariffs on China during a press briefing. In addition, she stated that the potential 80% tariffs on China is just a number that Trump “threw out there,” referencing a Truth Social post made by the President this morning.

Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt Holds Press Briefing

Furthermore, Leavitt said that the U.S. must see concessions from China in order to make progress on a trade deal. The two countries are set to begin talks this weekend in a highly anticipated meeting in Switzerland.

The S&P 500 is trading lower ahead of the event, erasing all of its morning gains.

Disclaimer & DisclosureReport an Issue
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nasdaq 100 Index Today: QQQ Stock Erases Gains Ahead of U.S.-China Meeting
data.description:
The Nasdaq 100 Index (NDX) is set to cap off an eventful week. After opening the Friday session higher by 0.40%, the technology index is now in the red.

Ahead of the weekend, all eyes are on the U.S.-China meeting in Switzerland. This morning, President Trump posted on Truth Social that 80% tariffs on China “seem right,” adding that the tariff level will ultimately be determined by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. Trump also said that China should open its market to the U.S., which would benefit both parties.

Trump’s tariffs have created much uncertainty surrounding the economy. Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams noted that he expects slowing growth, higher inflation, and higher unemployment as a result of these policies.

The Nasdaq 100 is down by 0.18% at the time of writing, bringing its weekly return to 0.19%.

Which Stocks are Moving the Nasdaq 100?

Let’s take a look at TipRank’s Nasdaq 100 Heatmap, which illustrates the stocks that have contributed to the index’s price action.

Most stocks within the technology and communication services sectors are adding on to the Nasdaq 100’s loss. However, Tesla (TSLA) and Texas Instruments (TXN) are performing quite well today with gains of 4.43% and 3.18%, respectively.

QQQ Stock Moves Lower with the Nasdaq 100

The Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) is an exchange-traded fund designed to track the movement of the Nasdaq 100.

Wall Street is quite bullish on QQQ stock. During the past three months, analysts have issued an average QQQ price target of $565.62 for the stocks within the index, implying upside of 16.02% from current prices. The 102 stocks in the QQQ carry 87 buy ratings, 15 hold ratings, and zero sell ratings.

data.title: U.S. New York Fed Expects 2.42% Q2 GDP Growth
data.description:
The Federal Bank Reserve of New York has updated its Nowcast model, forecasting 2.42% GDP growth for the second quarter. That figure increased from the forecast of 2.34% a week ago on May 2. The estimate was much higher at 2.72% on April 25.

The bank cited positive trade data as a major factor for the increase. Its model includes several inputs, including retail sales, industrial production, and the status of the labor market.

Keep up to date with the latest economic updates with TipRank’s Economic Indicators Dashboard.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. and Switzerland Agree to Accelerate Trade Talks
data.description:
Swiss President Karin Keller-Sutter has said that both the U.S. and Switzerland have agreed to accelerate trade talks. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are set to meet with Keller-Sutter and Economy Minister Guy Parmelin in a second meeting.

In 2024, the U.S. exported $25 billion of goods to Switzerland and imported $63.4 billion of goods, resulting in a $38.5 billion trade deficit.

The IShares MSCI Switzerland ETF (EWL) is trading higher on the news.

Disclaimer & DisclosureReport an Issue
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: China Firmly Opposes Abuse of Reciprocal Tariffs, Says Commerce Minister Wang
data.description:
China’s Commerce Minister, Wang Wentao, has advocated against the U.S. abusing the power of reciprocal tariffs ahead of the weekend meeting between the two nations. Wang also stated that a potential trade deal would be based on a full assessment of factors in a statement from the China Commerce Ministry.

U.S. and China Exchange Remarks Ahead of Weekend Meeting

This morning, Trump floated the idea of 80% tariffs on China in a Truth Social post, saying that it “seems right.” He added that the tariff rate will ultimately be decided by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

Despite the tariff drama, the iShares China Large-Cap ETF (FXI) has returned 13.24% year-to-date.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump: '80% Tariff on China seems right!'
data.description:
In a new Truth Social post, President Trump stated that “80% Tariff on China seems right! Up to Scott B.” Over the weekend, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and U.S. Trade Rep. Jamieson Greer will meet with China Vice Premier He Lifeng in a highly anticipated meeting.

Trump Teases Additional Trade Deals

In a separate post, Trump noted that “Many Trade Deals in the hopper, all good (GREAT!) ones!” Yesterday, the U.S. signed its first trade agreement with the UK while maintaining its 10% baseline tariff.

The S&P 500 closed higher on the news and is set to open in the green today.

data.title: BYD Seeks Overseas Dominance with a Target to Sell 50% Vehicles Outside China by 2030
data.description:
Chinese automaker BYD Company (BYDDF) (BYDDY) is seeking to dominate the overseas markets, with a target to sell half of its vehicles outside the Chinese market by 2030, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter. The company is trying to rapidly expand in the international market and replicate its impressive growth in China over the past five years, backed by the demand for its affordable EVs (electric vehicles) and hybrids. 

BYD Aims for Robust International Expansion

Reportedly, BYD executives have highlighted the 2030 target to investors in small groups since late 2024. In particular, the company indicated that its European expansion is critical for achieving its goal.

The Chinese company's target seems ambitious, even if we consider its stellar growth pace. This is because about 90% of the 4.27 million vehicles that BYD sold last year were from China. Meanwhile, BYD sold 417,204 units overseas in 2024, reflecting nearly 72% growth year over year.

Following some setbacks, BYD has revived its European business through various initiatives, including the expansion of its dealer network and the hiring of executives who are familiar with the local markets. BYD’s European sales, including the U.K., more than tripled in Q1 2025 to more than 37,000 vehicles from about 8,500 in the prior-year quarter.

BYD is already giving Tesla (TSLA) tough competition in the EV space. If the company meets its 2030 international expansion growth, it will emerge as a major threat to global players like Toyota and Volkswagen.

Is BYDDF a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street is highly bullish on BYD stock, with a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 12 unanimous Buys. The average BYDDF stock price target of $66.21 implies 33% upside potential. BYDDF stock has rallied about 47% year-to-date.
